questions.explanation: Substitute the first equation into the second: $3x + (2x + 1) = 11 \Rightarrow 5x + 1 = 11 \Rightarrow 5x = 10 \Rightarrow x = 2$. Substitute ${x=2}${ into }${y=2x+1}$: ${y = 2(2) + 1 = 5}${. Thus, }${x=2, y=5}$.

Subtract the first equation from the second: $(5x+y) - (2x+y) = 13 - 7 \Rightarrow 3x = 6 \Rightarrow x = 2$. Substitute ${x=2}$ into ${2x+y=7}$: $2(2)+y=7 \Rightarrow 4+y=7 \Rightarrow y=3$. Thus, ${x=2, y=3}$.

Add the two equations: $(4x+2y) + (x-2y) = 10 + (-5) \Rightarrow 5x = 5 \Rightarrow x = 1$. Substitute ${x=1}$ into ${4x+2y=10}$: $4(1)+2y=10 \Rightarrow 4+2y=10 \Rightarrow 2y=6 \Rightarrow y=3$. Thus, ${x=1, y=3}$.

Multiply the second equation by 3: $3(2x + y) = 3(5) \Rightarrow 6x + 3y = 15$. Subtract the first equation from this new equation: $(6x + 3y) - (x + 3y) = 15 - 10 \Rightarrow 5x = 5 \Rightarrow x = 1$. Substitute ${x=1}$ into ${x+3y=10}$: $1+3y=10 \Rightarrow 3y=9 \Rightarrow y=3$. Thus, ${x=1, y=3}$.

Multiply the first equation by 3 (${9x + 6y = 36}${) and the second by 2 ($4x - 6y = -10}$). Add the resulting equations: $(9x+6y) + (4x-6y) = 36 + (-10) \Rightarrow 13x = 26 \Rightarrow x = 2$. Substitute ${x=2}$ into ${3x+2y=12}$: $3(2)+2y=12 \Rightarrow 6+2y=12 \Rightarrow 2y=6 \Rightarrow y=3$. Thus, ${x=2, y=3}$.

questions.questionText: The sum of two numbers is 15. Their difference is 3. What are the two numbers?
questions.explanation: Let the numbers be ${x}$ and ${y}$. The equations are ${x+y=15}$ and ${x-y=3}$. Adding these equations gives $2x=18 \Rightarrow x=9${. Substituting }${x=9}${ into }${x+y=15}$ gives $9+y=15 \Rightarrow y=6$. The numbers are 9 and 6.

questions.questionText: A shop sells pens and notebooks. 2 pens and 3 notebooks cost £13. 3 pens and 1 notebook cost £9. How much does one pen cost?
questions.explanation: Let ${p}$ be the cost of a pen and ${n}$ the cost of a notebook. The equations are ${2p+3n=13}${ (1) and $3p+n=9}$ (2). From (2), ${n=9-3p}$. Substitute this into (1): $2p+3(9-3p)=13 \Rightarrow 2p+27-9p=13 \Rightarrow -7p=-14 \Rightarrow p=2$. Therefore, one pen costs £2.00.

Substitute ${y=3x-1}$ into the second equation: $2x - (3x - 1) = 3 \Rightarrow 2x - 3x + 1 = 3 \Rightarrow -x + 1 = 3 \Rightarrow -x = 2 \Rightarrow x = -2$. Substitute ${x=-2}${ into }${y=3x-1}$: ${y = 3(-2) - 1 = -6 - 1 = -7}${. Thus, }${x=-2, y=-7}$.

Rearrange the first equation to ${5x - 2y = 11}$. Multiply this by 2: $2(5x - 2y) = 2(11) \Rightarrow 10x - 4y = 22$. Add this new equation to ${3x + 4y = 17}${: }$(10x-4y) + (3x+4y) = 22+17 \Rightarrow 13x = 39 \Rightarrow x = 3${. Substitute }${x=3}${ into $5x - 2y = 11}$: $5(3) - 2y = 11 \Rightarrow 15 - 2y = 11 \Rightarrow -2y = -4 \Rightarrow y = 2$. Thus, ${x=3, y=2}$.

questions.questionText:
Solve the simultaneous equations:
${x/2 + y/3 = 3}$
${x - y/2 = 2.5}$
questions.explanation: Multiply the first equation by 6 to clear denominators: $6(x/2 + y/3) = 6(3) \Rightarrow 3x + 2y = 18$. Multiply the second equation by 2 to clear denominators: $2(x - y/2) = 2(2.5) \Rightarrow 2x - y = 5$. From ${2x-y=5}${, we get }${y=2x-5}${. Substitute this into $3x+2y=18}$: $3x+2(2x-5)=18 \Rightarrow 3x+4x-10=18 \Rightarrow 7x=28 \Rightarrow x=4$. Substitute ${x=4}${ into }${y=2x-5}$: ${y=2(4)-5=8-5=3}${. Thus, }${x=4, y=3}$.

questions.questionText: A rectangle has a perimeter of 40 cm. The length of the rectangle is 4 cm more than its width. Find the length and width of the rectangle.
questions.explanation: Let ${l}$ be the length and ${w}$ be the width. The equations are $2l+2w=40 \Rightarrow l+w=20${ (1) and }${l=w+4}${ (2). Substitute (2) into (1): }$(w+4)+w=20 \Rightarrow 2w+4=20 \Rightarrow 2w=16 \Rightarrow w=8${. Substitute }${w=8}${ into }${l=w+4}${: }${l=8+4=12}$. Thus, Length = 12 cm, Width = 8 cm.

questions.questionText: The current age of a father is three times the age of his son. In 5 years, the father's age will be two and a half times his son's age. Find their current ages.
questions.explanation: Let $F${ be the father's current age and $S}$ be the son's current age. Equations are ${F=3S}${ (1) and $F+5=2.5(S+5)}$ (2). Substitute (1) into (2): $3S+5=2.5S+12.5 \Rightarrow 0.5S=7.5 \Rightarrow S=15${. Substitute $S=15}$ into ${F=3S}${: $F=3(15)=45}$. Thus, Father = 45, Son = 15.
